title: Sourced origin notes on at least 100 entries (open/PD/CC sources, non-partisan)
context: Short, sourced origin notes add the 'why is it called that?' dimension to the corpus. Each note must be: written non-partisan and descriptively (no adjudicating sovereignty or contested names); sourced from at least 1 open, public-domain, or CC-licensed reference; and reviewed by the cultural/linguistic reviewer before publication. Contested and dual names must be described with attribution to the communities using each name, not by declaring a 'correct' one. No paywalled or unlicensed text may be copied. Depends on place-name-pronunciation-data-002.
objective: Write and commit sourced OriginNote records for at least 100 Place entries in the corpus, each citing at least 1 open/PD/CC source, handling contested/dual names non-partisanly, and passing cultural/linguistic review before publication.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 100 OriginNote records are committed, each associated with a Place entry in the corpus.
Each OriginNote cites at least 1 open-access, public-domain, or CC-licensed source; no paywalled or unlicensed text is copied.
Contested or dual names are handled descriptively: both/all names and their communities are attributed; no 'correct' name is declared.
Every OriginNote carries a neutralityNote field explaining how contested framing was avoided (if applicable).
All OriginNote records carry a reviewStatus and culturalReviewBy field signed by the cultural/linguistic reviewer.
Every note passes cultural/linguistic review before publication; any note failing review is revised or excluded.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass with the OriginNote records present.
output: At least 100 OriginNote records committed via PR, each sourced from open/PD/CC references and signed off by the cultural/linguistic reviewer.
